command.sfs.access-mode.list.description: List SFS file-system read-only and read-write settings. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:rwinfo:get.
command.sfs.access-mode.set-read-only.de: Set an SFS file system to read-only. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:ro:set.
command.sfs.access-mode.set-read-write.d: Set an SFS file system to read-write. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:rw:set.
command.sfs.ad-domain.join.description: Join an SFS file system to an AD domain. The file system must use CIFS, be available, and pass Keytab and Keytab MD5 validation before joining; query the AD configuration afterwards to confirm success. AD support is a public beta for whitelisted users and is available for trial only in North China 2 AZ1.
command.sfs.ad-domain.leave.description: Remove an SFS file system from its AD domain. The file system must already be joined to AD; query AD details afterwards to confirm removal. AD support is a public beta for whitelisted users and is available for trial only in North China 2 AZ1.
command.sfs.ad-domain.show.description: Retrieve the AD-domain configuration for an SFS file system. The file system must already be joined to AD. AD support is a public beta for whitelisted users and is available for trial only in North China 2 AZ1.
command.sfs.ad-domain.update.description: Update the AD-domain configuration for an SFS file system. The file system must already be joined to AD. AD support is a public beta for whitelisted users and is available for trial only in North China 2 AZ1.
command.sfs.ad-domain.validate.descripti: Validate AD-domain Keytab evidence for an SFS file system. AD support is a public beta for whitelisted users and is available for trial only in North China 2 AZ1.
command.sfs.capacity.free.description: Retrieve an SFS file system's free capacity. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:capacity:get.
command.sfs.capacity.total.description: Retrieve an SFS file system's total capacity. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:capacity:get.
command.sfs.capacity.usage-rate.descript: Retrieve an SFS file system's capacity usage rate. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:capacity:get.
command.sfs.capacity.used.description: Retrieve an SFS file system's used capacity. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:capacity:get.
command.sfs.file-system-vpc.bind.descrip: Bind a permission group to an SFS file system's VPC. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools and requires the file-system VPC not to have a permission group already bound. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:permission:bind.
command.sfs.file-system-vpc.change-permi: Replace the permission group bound to an SFS file system's VPC. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools; the VPC must already have a different permission group bound. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:permission:change.
command.sfs.file-system-vpc.list.descrip: List VPC and permission-group bindings for an SFS file system. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:bindingInfo:get.
command.sfs.file-system-vpc.unbind.descr: Unbind a permission group from an SFS file system's VPC. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools and requires the named permission group to be bound already. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:permission:unbind.
command.sfs.file-system.create.descripti: Create one or more SFS file systems. The VPC and subnet must be available, the region must support the selected storage type, and encrypted creation requires an available symmetric KMS key. Capacity defaults to 500-32768 GB and account quota defaults to 50 TB. Subscription creation requires cycle count and type with a maximum term of three years. 4.0 resource pools bind the default permission group automatically. Shanghai 7, Nanjing 3, and Chengdu 4 support only 500 GB, 1 TB, 2 TB, 3 TB, 5 TB, 10 TB, 20 TB, or 30 TB sizes. azName is equivalent to zoneName, and the portal identifies azName as the canonical field. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:shares:create.
command.sfs.file-system.delete.descripti: Delete or unsubscribe from an SFS file system. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:shares:delete.
List SFS file systems by billing mode. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:shares:list. VPCE share paths are exposed for 4.0 availability-zone resource pools; standard bare-metal clients must use the VPCE share path, while existing VM and container mount fields are unchanged.
command.sfs.file-system.list-by-protocol: List SFS file systems by protocol. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:shares:list. VPCE share paths are exposed for 4.0 availability-zone resource pools; standard bare-metal clients must use the VPCE share path, while existing VM and container mount fields are unchanged.
command.sfs.file-system.list-by-storage-: List SFS file systems by storage type. VPCE share paths are exposed for 4.0 availability-zone resource pools; standard bare-metal clients must use the VPCE share path, while existing VM and container mount fields are unchanged.
command.sfs.file-system.list-by-vpc.desc: List SFS file systems in a VPC. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:fsInVpc:list. VPCE share paths are exposed for 4.0 availability-zone resource pools; standard bare-metal clients must use the VPCE share path, while existing VM and container mount fields are unchanged.
command.sfs.file-system.list-opened.desc: List SFS file systems opened by the tenant. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:shares:list. VPCE share paths are exposed for 4.0 availability-zone resource pools; standard bare-metal clients must use the VPCE share path, while existing VM and container mount fields are unchanged.
command.sfs.file-system.list.description: List SFS file systems in an availability zone. VPCE share paths are exposed for 4.0 availability-zone resource pools; standard bare-metal clients must use the VPCE share path, while existing VM and container mount fields are unchanged.
command.sfs.file-system.rename.descripti: Rename an SFS file system. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:shares:put.
command.sfs.file-system.renew.descriptio: Renew a subscription SFS file system. Renewal supports only subscription file systems where onDemand is false. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:shares:update.
command.sfs.file-system.resize.descripti: Increase an SFS file system's capacity. SFS supports expansion only, not shrinking. Total capacity remains subject to the default 50 TB account quota and the default ten-file-system regional quota. Shanghai 7, Nanjing 3, and Chengdu 4 support only 500 GB, 1 TB, 2 TB, 3 TB, 5 TB, 10 TB, 20 TB, or 30 TB sizes. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:shares:update.
command.sfs.file-system.show-by-name.des: Retrieve an SFS file system by name. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:shares:get. VPCE share paths are exposed for 4.0 availability-zone resource pools; standard bare-metal clients must use the VPCE share path, while existing VM and container mount fields are unchanged.
command.sfs.file-system.show.description: Retrieve an SFS file system by ID. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:shares:get. VPCE share paths are exposed for 4.0 availability-zone resource pools; standard bare-metal clients must use the VPCE share path, while existing VM and container mount fields are unchanged.
command.sfs.label.batch-bind.description: Bind labels to multiple SFS file systems. Only existing file systems can be labelled, with a one-to-two-minute wait after creation. Each file system supports at most 50 labels, keys must be unique per resource and request, an existing key updates its value, and missing account labels are created. Label management is available only in selected resource pools; consult the product capability map.
command.sfs.label.list-by-file-system.de: List labels bound to an SFS file system. Label management is available only in selected resource pools; consult the product capability map.
command.sfs.label.update.description: Bind or unbind labels on an SFS file system. Only existing file systems can be labelled, with a one-to-two-minute wait after creation. Each file system supports at most 50 labels, keys must be unique per resource and request, an existing key updates its value, and missing account labels are created. Label management is available only in selected resource pools; consult the product capability map.
command.sfs.mount-point.add.description: Add one mount point to an SFS file system. This API applies only to 3.0 resource pools, supports adding one mount point at a time, and permits at most 20 mount points per file system. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:vpcs:bind.
command.sfs.mount-point.delete.descripti: Delete one mount point from an SFS file system. Only one specified mount point can be deleted. This API applies only to 3.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:vpcs:unbind.
command.sfs.mount-point.list.description: List mount points for an SFS file system. This API applies only to 3.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:vpcs:list.
command.sfs.permission-group.create.desc: Create an SFS permission group. A user can create at most 20 permission groups per resource pool. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:permission:create.
command.sfs.permission-group.delete.desc: Delete an SFS permission group. The default permission group cannot be deleted, and a group bound to a file system cannot be deleted. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:permission:delete.
command.sfs.permission-group.list.descri: List SFS permission groups or retrieve one permission group. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:permission:get.
command.sfs.permission-group.update.desc: Update an SFS permission group. The default permission group cannot be modified. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:permission:update.
command.sfs.permission-rule.create.descr: Create an SFS permission-group rule. Each permission group supports at most 400 rules. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:rule:create.
command.sfs.permission-rule.delete.descr: Delete an SFS permission-group rule. The default permission-group rule cannot be deleted. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:rule:delete.
command.sfs.permission-rule.list.descrip: List SFS permission-group rules or retrieve one rule. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:rule:get.
command.sfs.permission-rule.update.descr: Update an SFS permission-group rule. Rule priority ranges from 1 to 400, defaults to 1, must be unique, and resolves conflicting single-IP and subnet rules; authorised addresses must also be unique. This API applies only to 4.0 resource pools. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:rule:update.
command.sfs.pricing.quote-create.descrip: Quote a subscription SFS file-system order. Pricing inquiry supports only subscription resources where onDemand is false.
command.sfs.pricing.quote-renewal.descri: Quote a subscription SFS file-system renewal. Pricing inquiry supports only subscription resources where onDemand is false.
command.sfs.pricing.quote-resize.descrip: Quote a subscription SFS file-system expansion. Pricing inquiry supports only subscription resources where onDemand is false.
command.sfs.quota.capacity.description: Retrieve SFS capacity quota usage in a region. The default regional capacity quota is 50 TB; request an adjustment through a support ticket when needed. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:capacityQuota:get.
command.sfs.quota.count.description: Retrieve SFS file-system count quota usage in a region. The default per-region file-system count quota is ten and can be raised to 20 by request; availability zones in a multi-AZ resource pool share its total quota. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:quantityQuota:get.
command.sfs.quota.vpc.description: Retrieve VPC quota usage for an SFS file system. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:vpcsQuota:get.
command.sfs.region.list.description: List regions that support SFS. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:region:list.
command.sfs.replication.create.descripti: Create cross-region replication between two SFS file systems. Replication is supported only between Southwest 1 AZ1 and North China 2 AZ1, between existing available file systems in different resource pools with the same storage and protocol types. The destination must have overwrite protection disabled; read-only destination permission rules reduce the risk of replicated data overwriting concurrent writes. This trial is limited to whitelisted users.
command.sfs.replication.delete.descripti: Delete an SFS cross-region replication configuration.
command.sfs.replication.list.description: List cross-region replication details for an SFS file system.
command.sfs.replication.set-protection.d: Set replication overwrite protection for an SFS file system. Overwrite protection defaults to true, and the resource pool must support cross-region replication.
command.sfs.storage-type.list.descriptio: List SFS storage types supported by a region. Calling as an IAM subaccount or delegated identity requires sfs:sfstype:info.
command.sfs.subdirectory.create.descript: Create a subdirectory in an SFS file system. This API is currently available only in East China 1, and directory depth cannot exceed 1,000 levels.
name: Scalable File Service

parameter.sfs.ad-domain.update.keytab_md: MD5 digest of the Keytab file content; supply it together with the Keytab content.

parameter.sfs.file-system-vpc.bind.vpc_i: VPC ID associated with the SFS file system.

parameter.sfs.file-system-vpc.unbind.reg: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.file-system-vpc.unbind.vpc: VPC ID associated with the SFS file system.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.az_name: Availability-zone name. It is equivalent to zoneName; the portal identifies azName as the canonical field.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.client_: Unique client token retained for 24 hours and used to make the order request idempotent.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.cycle_c: Subscription cycle count: 1-36 months or 1-3 years, with a maximum total term of three years.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.cycle_t: Subscription cycle type: year or month.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.is_encr: Whether the file system is encrypted; default is false. Encrypted creation requires an available symmetric KMS key.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.kms_uui: KMS key UUID; required when encryption is enabled and limited to an available symmetric KMS key.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.on_dema: Billing mode; default is true for on-demand billing. Cycle type and count are required when false for subscription billing.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.order_n: Number of identically configured file systems to create, from 1 to 10 and subject to quota; default is 1.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.project: Enterprise project ID; default is 0 for the default project.
Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.sfs_nam: SFS file-system name containing 2-255 letters, digits, or hyphens; it cannot start with a digit or hyphen or end with a hyphen.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.sfs_pro: File-system protocol: nfs for Linux or cifs for Windows.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.sfs_siz: SFS file-system capacity in GB, subject to the operation's quota and resource-pool size constraints.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.sfs_typ: SFS storage type: capacity for standard storage or performance for performance storage.
parameter.sfs.file-system.create.subnet_: Subnet ID in the selected VPC; required in a 3.0 resource pool but not unconditionally required for every resource-pool version.
VPC ID used to create the file system.
parameter.sfs.file-system.delete.client_: Unique client token retained for 24 hours and used to make the order request idempotent.

parameter.sfs.file-system.list-by-billin: Billing mode: true for on-demand billing or false for subscription billing; default is true.

parameter.sfs.file-system.renew.client_t: Unique client token retained for 24 hours and used to make the order request idempotent.

parameter.sfs.file-system.renew.region.d: Region ID used to scope the request.

parameter.sfs.file-system.show-by-name.r: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.file-system.show-by-name.s: SFS file-system name containing 2-255 letters, digits, or hyphens; it cannot start with a digit or hyphen or end with a hyphen.
parameter.sfs.file-system.show.region.de: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.label.batch-bind.label_lis: JSON array of label objects, subject to the operation's per-file-system and unique-key limits.
parameter.sfs.label.batch-bind.region.de: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.label.batch-bind.sfs_uid_l: JSON array of SFS file-system UIDs.

parameter.sfs.label.update.label_list.de: JSON array of label objects whose nested operateType is BIND or UNBIND, subject to the per-file-system and unique-key limits.
parameter.sfs.label.update.region.descri: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.mount-point.add.region.des: Region ID used to scope the request.

parameter.sfs.permission-rule.create.per: Unique rule priority from 1 to 400, where 1 is the highest priority and the default.
parameter.sfs.permission-rule.create.reg: Region ID used to scope the request.

parameter.sfs.permission-rule.update.reg: Region ID used to scope the request.

parameter.sfs.pricing.quote-create.order: Number of file systems included in the subscription price inquiry, from 1 to 50; default is 1.
parameter.sfs.pricing.quote-create.regio: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.pricing.quote-create.sfs_s: SFS file-system capacity in GB, subject to the operation's quota and resource-pool size constraints.
parameter.sfs.pricing.quote-create.volum: Quoted storage type: hdd for standard SFS or nvme for performance SFS.

parameter.sfs.pricing.quote-renewal.regi: Region ID used to scope the request.

parameter.sfs.quota.count.region.descrip: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.quota.vpc.region.descripti: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.replication.create.destina: Region ID containing the destination SFS file system.
parameter.sfs.replication.create.source_: Region ID containing the source SFS file system.

parameter.sfs.replication.list.page_no.d: Page number, starting at 1; default is 1.
parameter.sfs.replication.list.page_size: Number of results per page, from 1 to 50; default is 10.
parameter.sfs.replication.list.region.de: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.replication.set-protection: Whether replication overwrite protection is enabled; default is true and it must be false before creating replication to this destination.

parameter.sfs.storage-type.list.page_siz: Number of results per page, from 1 to 50; default is 10.
parameter.sfs.storage-type.list.region.d: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.storage-type.list.sfs_type: SFS storage type: capacity for standard storage or performance for performance storage.
parameter.sfs.subdirectory.create.region: Region ID used to scope the request.
parameter.sfs.subdirectory.create.subdir: Absolute SFS subdirectory path beginning with /, containing letters, digits, underscores, hyphens, or Chinese characters, with 1-63 characters per level.
